title: Test plan (stub)
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.2 as the stub contract for QA-authored test plans. Stub scope: test_id / covers / commands / expected per test. Phase 4 (specs-agent-driven plans, Arazzo-backed) and Phase 5 (generator-emitted skeletons) extend this schema additively — see agents/qa/skills/qa-authoring/SKILL.md §Deferred integration. The schema validates the YAML frontmatter block of /product/test-plans/FEAT-YYYY-NNNN.md; the prose body below the frontmatter is human-authored and not schema-governed.
description: Machine-readable shape of a test plan authored by the QA agent (qa-authoring skill). One plan per feature; one entry in `tests` per behavior the plan covers. The full plan file is the .md at /product/test-plans/FEAT-YYYY-NNNN.md, whose YAML frontmatter validates against this schema.

schema_version.description: Version of this stub contract. Bumped on breaking changes to the schema. Phase 3 stub is v1.

feature_correlation_id.description: Feature-level correlation ID the plan covers. Task-level IDs are not valid here — a plan is authored per feature, not per task.

tests.description: Ordered list of tests covering the feature's acceptance criteria. One entry per covered behavior by default; feature-scope overrides on qa_authoring cardinality (WU 2.10) may collapse the count.

test_id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this test within the plan. Must be unique across the plan's `tests` array (uniqueness is a skill-level discipline — JSON Schema cannot express cross-item uniqueness on a nested field directly). Stability is load-bearing: qa-regression keys regression artifacts on (implementation_task_correlation_id, test_id), so renaming a test_id in place would orphan open regressions.

covers.description: Reference to the acceptance-criteria fragment this test validates. A direct quote, heading path, or identifier from the feature's spec — precise enough that a human reading the plan can locate the covered behavior in the source spec without guessing.

commands.description: Executable step list the qa-execution skill runs for this test. In the Phase 3 stub, each command is a free-form shell string; Phase 4 replaces this with richer Arazzo-backed step structures (see skill's §Deferred integration).

expected.description: Success predicate for the test — a prose statement describing what qa-execution confirms. The Phase 3 stub is deliberately textual; Phase 4 structures this as a machine-evaluable predicate.
